name: now-mcp
description: Fluent-native MCP server for ServiceNow: live read/observe + data tools for Claude, paired with auto-injected now-sdk/now-mcp workflow rules.

instance_url.title: ServiceNow instance URL
instance_url.description: Your instance URL, e.g. https://dev123456.service-now.com — this is all most people need. Fill this + Username + Password and leave 'Config file' empty. (Only skip these three if you use a Config file instead, for OAuth / multiple instances.)

username.title: Username
username.description: ServiceNow username (basic auth) for the instance URL above.

password.title: Password
password.description: ServiceNow password. Masked here and saved to your OS keychain, never written to disk in plaintext.

read_only.title: Read-only (type false to allow writes)
read_only.description: Type exactly false to allow create/update/delete. Leave empty or type true to stay read-only (the safe default — blocks all writes). Only false/no/off/0 enable writes; anything unrecognized stays read-only for safety.

config_path.title: Config file (OPTIONAL — leave empty for a single instance)
config_path.description: Leave EMPTY if you filled in URL + Username + Password above. Only for OAuth / multiple instances / now-sdk pairing: create a YAML file anywhere on your machine and put its absolute path here (it then overrides the fields above). A ready-to-copy annotated template ships with the plugin at config/sn-credential.example.yaml (also viewable at https://github.com/owenljy/foundry-suite/blob/main/config/sn-credential.example.yaml) — copy it, fill in your instance(s), and point this field at your copy.
